raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding a dispute denial by Barclays related to a charge for construction services that were misrepresented and effectively resulted in duplicate payment. 

The transaction in question is a charge in the amount of {$7800.00}, which was paid using my Barclays credit card for windows and exterior doors related to a residential construction project. 

I am the homeowner and borrower on a construction loan for the property located in XXXX XXXX, Florida. Under the construction contract, the contractor was responsible for ordering and installing these materials. At the time of the charge, I paid the contractor directly with my credit card as an accommodation so work could proceed. 

However, under the construction loan draw schedule with my lender, the contractor was later reimbursed through the construction loan for the same work category ( windows and exterior doors ). This means the contractor effectively received payment through the construction loan draw process while I remained responsible for the credit card charge. 

When I realized that the contractor had been reimbursed through the construction draw schedule and had not reimbursed me for the charge I made on my credit card, I filed a dispute with Barclays for the {$7800.00} transaction. I provided documentation showing that : The charge was made for windows and exterior doors related to a construction project The contractor later received construction draw funds related to that same work category I personally paid the charge and was never reimbursed The contractor retained the benefit of both payments Despite this documentation, Barclays denied the dispute and concluded that they could not issue a permanent credit. As a result, I am now being held responsible for a charge that represents a duplicate payment for the same construction work. 

I believe the dispute investigation conducted by Barclays was incomplete and did not fully consider the context of the transaction or the documentation provided. This was not a typical retail purchase ; it was a construction-related payment where the contractor was later funded through a lender draw for the same category of work.

In addition, I successfully disputed a separate charge with another credit card issuer related to the same contractor, which resulted in a permanent credit being issued after review of the circumstances. This indicates that other financial institutions reviewing similar documentation recognized the underlying issue.
